codeReview.summary: Fail. The reviewed fix commit is test-only and does not resolve the prior blocking findings from m4-fix-backfill-runtime-execution-coverage-and-strict-idempotency. After comparing both diffs, this feature introduces SwitcherCrossFlow tests but does not touch the backfill runtime guard/monotonic logic or its runtime-coverage tests that were previously marked blocking.

issues.description: The fix commit only adds cross-surface switcher tests and project wiring; it does not implement code changes for the prior failed backfill feature blockers. This does not constitute a fix for the earlier blocking review findings.
Original blocking area from prior review (strict monotonic backfill cursor behavior) is not modified by this fix commit. Therefore this rerun fix does not directly resolve that prior blocker.

Original blocking area from prior review (runtime execution coverage expectations for refreshAll/runScheduledBackfillIfNeeded success/failure paths) is not modified by this fix commit, so prior blocker resolution is not demonstrated by this feature diff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Handoff commit ID does not satisfy the skill's handoff discipline requirement for full 40-char SHA.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff file lists "commitId": "7518bb9" while .factory/skills/switcher-integration-worker/SKILL.md requires a full SHA-1 commit ID.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker marked followedProcedure=true without showing required context-read evidence in commandsRun.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md requires concrete read-evidence for required context when followedProcedure=true; handoff verification.commandsRun only shows xcodebuild test invocations and no context-read commands.

summary: Reviewed transcript skeleton, handoff, and both diffs (original failed feature commit 65d2570f11512848055771e41b2277bd11852fa3 and fix commit 7518bb9b72f9348e4e193623afcdb8a2cf4a428b). Determination: fail, because this fix is orthogonal (switcher test additions) and does not resolve the prior blocking backfill issues documented in the referenced failed review.
